description:
You are a release-manager who has just cloned a brand-new project called “myapp” into the default user account ( /home/user ).  
Before the first automated deployment can run, CI/CD expects a very precise set of environment-management artefacts to exist on disk.

Your job is to build that filesystem structure and fill in every file exactly as specified below.  
Nothing more, nothing less.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Directory layout that must exist when you are finished
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
/home/user/releases/                    (already exists – do not create)
/home/user/releases/myapp/              (create)
/home/user/releases/myapp/env/          (create)
/home/user/releases/myapp/env/development/   (create)
/home/user/releases/myapp/env/staging/       (create)
/home/user/releases/myapp/env/production/    (create)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2. Template file: /home/user/releases/myapp/.env.example
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Create the file and copy–paste the six variable keys **exactly** as shown, each on its own line, with the value left intentionally empty:

DB_HOST=
DB_PORT=
DB_USER=
DB_PASS=
API_KEY=
LOG_LEVEL=

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3. Environment-specific “.env” files
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Inside each of the three sub-directories, create a file literally named “.env”.  
Populate the files with the exact key/value pairs shown below (same order, one per line, UNIX newline at the end of the last line).

3 a. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/development/.env
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=devuser
DB_PASS=devpass123
API_KEY=DEV-23ab9
LOG_LEVEL=debug

3 b. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/staging/.env
DB_HOST=staging-db.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=stageuser
DB_PASS=stagepass123
API_KEY=STG-584af
LOG_LEVEL=info

3 c. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/production/.env
DB_HOST=prod-db.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=produser
DB_PASS=prodpass123
API_KEY=PRD-9f3cd
LOG_LEVEL=warning

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4. Consolidated CSV: /home/user/releases/myapp/env/all_envs_summary.csv
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Generate a CSV that concatenates **all** variables from the three .env files.  
Required format:

• First row is a header literally:  ENV,KEY,VALUE  
• Subsequent rows are grouped by environment in the order: development, staging, production.  
• Within each group, preserve the key order that appears in the corresponding .env.  
• Use a UNIX newline at the end of the last row and do not surround values with quotes.

Example of the first few lines (truncated for brevity):

ENV,KEY,VALUE
development,DB_HOST,localhost
development,DB_PORT,5432
…(continue for the remaining 4 keys)…  
staging,DB_HOST,staging-db.internal
…

Exactly 19 lines total: 1 header + (3 envs × 6 keys).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5. Deployment variable log: /home/user/releases/myapp/deploy_vars.log
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CI/CD will scan this plaintext log to ensure that only non-sensitive variables were exported during pipeline execution.

Rules and required format:
a) Log ONLY the following keys for each environment:  DB_HOST, DB_PORT, API_KEY, LOG_LEVEL  
   (Do NOT log DB_USER nor DB_PASS.)
b) Log for staging first, then production. Development is intentionally excluded.  
c) For determinism, every log entry must begin with exactly this hard-coded timestamp string:
   [2023-01-01 00:00:00]
d) Immediately after the timestamp, write a single space, then the literal text  ENV=staging  (or production), another single space, and finally the literal bash command you would have run, e.g.:

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=staging export DB_HOST=staging-db.internal

e) Produce one line per exported variable (there are 4 variables per environment × 2 environments = 8 lines total).  
f) Preserve the exact key order given in rule (a).

When you’re done, the very last character of the file must be a UNIX newline.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6. Verification notes
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Automated tests will fail if:

• Any directory or filename deviates from the paths above.  
• A file has extra or missing whitespace / newlines.  
• deploy_vars.log contains any key other than the four allowed ones, a wrong timestamp, or wrong order.  
• all_envs_summary.csv has an incorrect number of lines or wrong ordering.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Important
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
You do NOT need to run the project; only create and populate the files.  
You do NOT have sudo rights. Everything must live under /home/user.  
Do NOT hard-code your own user path; always use the exact absolute paths given.  
Feel free to inspect existing directories before creating new ones.

Good luck—once the artefacts match the spec, the deployment pipeline will pass.
truth:
Expected final filesystem objects and their exact contents (all end with a single “
” newline unless otherwise noted):

1. /home/user/releases/myapp/.env.example  
DB_HOST=
DB_PORT=
DB_USER=
DB_PASS=
API_KEY=
LOG_LEVEL=


2. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/development/.env  
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=devuser
DB_PASS=devpass123
API_KEY=DEV-23ab9
LOG_LEVEL=debug


3. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/staging/.env  
DB_HOST=staging-db.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=stageuser
DB_PASS=stagepass123
API_KEY=STG-584af
LOG_LEVEL=info


4. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/production/.env  
DB_HOST=prod-db.internal
DB_PORT=5432
DB_USER=produser
DB_PASS=prodpass123
API_KEY=PRD-9f3cd
LOG_LEVEL=warning


5. /home/user/releases/myapp/env/all_envs_summary.csv  
ENV,KEY,VALUE

development,DB_HOST,localhost

development,DB_PORT,5432

development,DB_USER,devuser

development,DB_PASS,devpass123

development,API_KEY,DEV-23ab9

development,LOG_LEVEL,debug

staging,DB_HOST,staging-db.internal

staging,DB_PORT,5432

staging,DB_USER,stageuser

staging,DB_PASS,stagepass123

staging,API_KEY,STG-584af

staging,LOG_LEVEL,info

production,DB_HOST,prod-db.internal

production,DB_PORT,5432

production,DB_USER,produser

production,DB_PASS,prodpass123

production,API_KEY,PRD-9f3cd

production,LOG_LEVEL,warning


6. /home/user/releases/myapp/deploy_vars.log  
[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=staging export DB_HOST=staging-db.internal

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=staging export DB_PORT=5432

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=staging export API_KEY=STG-584af

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=staging export LOG_LEVEL=info

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=production export DB_HOST=prod-db.internal

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=production export DB_PORT=5432

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=production export API_KEY=PRD-9f3cd

[2023-01-01 00:00:00] ENV=production export LOG_LEVEL=warning
